name: The White House
subject: Enforcing the law—at the border and within
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9f3e2fbf9a165da194373cb456ed6a757a860a3484be010dfcd17b2972679a151f74ddc94b04e0c927389518f7d6c6fcadbbbc250ad0e068

Enforcing the law—at the border and within

“California sanctuary policies put the entire Nation at risk,” President Donald J. Trump said while visiting the U.S.-Mexico border this week. “They’re the best friend of the criminal.”

Sanctuary city policies aren’t about protecting law-abiding immigrants. Rather, the term “sanctuary city” generally refers to localities that refuse to cooperate with lawful Federal immigration enforcement. When a suspected illegal alien is arrested on criminal charges, these jurisdictions will often release the suspect quickly before Federal officials can arrive.

After visiting Southern California to examine border wall prototypes this week, President Trump plans to host a roundtable with law enforcement officials to discuss the threat from these sanctuary cities. His goal is to ensure that Federal law is enforced both at the border and inside our Nation.

“I want to thank ICE and the Border Patrol agents for their incredible work and their incredible bravery,” the President said on Tuesday. “It’s a dangerous job. It’s a tough job.”

Watch:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9f3e2fbf9a165da14258405be2b3c54f8c7d150f5ac1e107da7ddebe6911976d023ec63893f02cda20a72a3596b900ebfd5cf473e48ab2ed
President Trump reviews border wall prototypes in California.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9f3e2fbf9a165da1572c33a1925be7dd7a70512737149e34039cf2d2a7640ae042bb3ab3ceeaf86afcfcef32cf1e27a596478529e3ab32b0
See examples of criminal aliens that California has released—with life-and-death consequences.

4 ways to rebuild American strength

As the Trump Administration rebuilds America’s strength and confidence at home, it is also restoring the Nation’s standing abroad. Four principles drive President Trump’s approach:

- Fighting to rebuild our military: Last month, President Trump signed a two-year budget deal that raises the military’s budget caps for national defense to $700 billion for Fiscal Year 2018.
- Putting America first in our strategy: The President’s national security strategy emphasizes protecting the homeland, promoting prosperity, preserving peace through strength, and advancing American influence.
- Ending the threat of terror: Under the Trump Administration’s watch, ISIS has lost nearly 100 percent of its former caliphate.
- Deterring threats to the American people: The President has rallied world powers in support of increasing economic and diplomatic pressure on North Korea while taking aggressive action to confront threatening behavior from Iran.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9f3e2fbf9a165da11144202ad3d59e535de8f8eaf8b582298b5dad3908d44d2f57cc2f889fa47350caaed974040b7f793bc872d6a7f18609
Learn more about how America is confronting threats abroad like never before.

Talking tax cuts in Missouri

One of the most rewarding outcomes of tax reform is listening to the stories of real Americans whose lives have been made better. This week in Missouri, President Trump heard many of these personal accounts as he met with workers and business leaders in St. Louis.

One person spoke about how tax changes have made it easier to grow his company through capital investments. Others talked about the $1,000 bonuses they were able to extend to hard-working employees after President Trump signed tax reform into law last December.

The President also toured a St. Louis Boeing facility that assembles U.S. fighter planes. "A few years ago, this plant was in very serious danger of shutting down, and we got it going again,” the President said. “We are keeping thousands of Americans employed right here in St. Louis.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9f3e2fbf9a165da1d9ce75b4ad3611dbb6cb29fe29507b60a04ac105f49376834043d84ead85407b9100c60c0c7367ce42b3facfc6a9defb
Watch the highlights of President Trump’s Boeing tour and roundtable with Missourians.

Photo of the Day

McGrath Academy of Irish Dance | March 15, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Emily Higgins)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will receive his daily intelligence briefing before having lunch with Vice President Mike Pence, Secretary of Defense Jim Mattis, and Secretary of Homeland Security Kirstjen Nielsen.

This afternoon, the Vice President will participate in a meeting with Japanese Foreign Minister Taro Kono.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9f3e2fbf9a165da1957c61cea841097d3d8f8dd0128031db9db43eabcc3dfa2c6d12be76d4e9fed384ff8d3512972ab7e26d99e5b67c9159

Contact the White House |

body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | **[dpwillis67@gmail.com:](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda3/196986789/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=1+-+NAMEEMAIL") [President Obama wants to end gerrymandering! Sign your name if you agree ->](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda2/196986789/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=2+-+President+Obama+wants+to+end+gerrymander")**
* * *
For years, Republicans have unfairly redrawn districts through a process called gerrymandering to rig the maps in their favor.

_That 's why President Barack Obama wants to put an END to gerrymandering:_ | [](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda1/196986789/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=3")
---
| [I AGREE WITH OBAMA ->](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda0/196986789/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=4+-+I+AGREE+WITH+OBAMAnbsprarr")
---
In Pennsylvania, Governor Tom Wolf rejected Republicans' gerrymandered map -- and supported a new, fair one in its place.

But Republicans are fighting to keep their old, rigged map. They want to keep drawing districts in their favor and shut voters out of the process.

Governor Wolf and President Obama are fighting back -- they support ending gerrymandering. But we need to know, are you with them?
**[Sign your name if you AGREE we must end gerrymandering:](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda7/196986789/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=5+-+Sign+your+name+if+you+AGREE+we+must+end+")**
| [I AGREE ->](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda6/196986789/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=6+-+I+AGREEnbsprarr")
---
In 2021, every state will have to draw new congressional maps. _And governors have the power to veto maps that shut voters out of their own democracy._

So if we're going to end gerrymandering, we need more Democratic governors like Tom Wolf who will fight to make sure politicians are held accountable to people, not special interests

**[We want to know just how many people are with us in the fight to end gerrymandering, please sign your name:](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf8b/73a2bc12/3ff8f44f/1fccfda5/196986789/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/agree-obama-gerrymandering?&utm_campaign=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180316_obama_clk_clq_act_ger_&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=7+-+We+want+to+know+just+how+many+people+are")**

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: The people's candidates vs. the political establishment
body:
#
Friends,
The 2018 election cycle is heating up. The results from special elections show a huge blue wave is building that will sweep hundreds of Democrats into office. We need to make sure that the candidates swept into office aren't the same old establishment politicians wearing a "blue team" shirt.
**Fortunately, in cities and states across the country, a new generation of leaders is rising up to take power from those billionaires who dominate our politics and our economy at the expense of the people.** These revolutionary leaders are running people-powered campaigns to transform our economy and our democracy so that America truly belongs to all of us.
Our Revolution has endorsed progressive champions--like Marie Newman in Illinois, Laura Moser in Texas, Joe Salazar in Colorado, and Steve Dunwoody in California--in competitive primary elections across the country.
**Our revolutionary candidates are up against a political establishment --even in the Democratic Party--that doesn't want revolutionary candidates to win.** Corporate-backed Democrats are throwing all their money and establishment support against their insurgent campaigns.
**Fortunately, our candidates have a secret weapon: you.** Just like Senator Bernie Sanders ran a people-powered campaign against the political establishment without corporate contributions, our candidates are looking to people like you to help them take on the political establishment in both parties.
[**Chip in $5 to support our work on behalf of revolutionary candidates.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7aca9/bed43eb/5f2dac74/75fe3c19/3028801095/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em180316-subb")
Your contributions will help support Get Out The Vote (GOTV) efforts that are the difference between winning and losing elections, progressing or regressing, electing bold leadership or settling for the status quo. Our Revolution will use your investment to make hundreds of thousands of calls and texts, and knock on tens of thousands of doors, to make sure we defeat the political establishment.
But we can't do it without your support.
[**Chip in $5 to help defeat the political establishment at the ballot box.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7aca9/bed43eb/5f2dac74/75fe3c19/3028801095/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em180316-subb")
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

name: Tina Smith
subject: More determined than ever.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
I'll carry the progressive legacy of this Senate seat forward, starting by listening to you. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49d0c/c1f7015/69a79baf/1143c4fb/1476873690/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tina-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-01-31_2018-welcome-recurring-1&refcode=ema_2018-01-31_2018-welcome-recurring-1&utm_campaign=2018-welcome-recurring-1&utm_medium=ema") Friend, I'm determined to win the fights ahead -- to help improve the lives of ALL Minnesotans and Americans. My parents instilled in me a strong belief that serving your community -- stepping up to help -- is a fundamental part of life. It's a value I've tried to instill in our kids. It's why I've always sought out work that helps people live with dignity and respect. After the 35-W bridge collapse, I worked across the aisle to honor the victims and get a new bridge built quickly. At Planned Parenthood, I fought to make sure women could make their own decisions about their health. And in state government, we built what's been called the "best-run state in America" by not shying away from a progressive agenda: asking the top 2 percent to pay their fair share in taxes, investing in our schools, and raising the minimum wage for hundreds of thousands of workers. Look, becoming a senator wasn't something I'd planned on. But it's not in my nature to shrink from a challenge. Instead, I'm going to work every day to seize this opportunity to help improve people's lives. And this November, with your help, we're going to keep this seat out of the hands of Trump enthusiasts who are already eyeing it. Make no mistake, if we don't win in Minnesota, Democrats won't be able to take back the Senate. During the next nine months, we're going to build a campaign worthy of Minnesota and the progressive legacy of this Senate seat -- a seat once held by Walter Mondale and Paul Wellstone. I'm going to honor that legacy every day by fighting for a progressive future: one that ensures opportunity is for all, not just a wealthy few. To realize that future and to win in November, we have to be in this together. Your voice will be essential to shaping my campaign this year and how I try to make progress in a divided Senate. **This campaign isn't going to be about me. It's going to be about us. So I'd love to hear from you: Which issues matter most to you in 2018?** Just click "**reply** " to respond directly to this email letting me know. I'm looking forward to hearing from you, and working together to be a force for good. I'm grateful you're a part of the team. Yours truly, Tina Smith
Senator for Minnesota | [JOIN OUR TEAM »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49d0c/c1f7015/69a79baf/1143c4fb/1476873690/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tina-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-01-31_2018-welcome-recurring-1&refcode=ema_2018-01-31_2018-welcome-recurring-1&utm_campaign=2018-welcome-recurring-1&utm_medium=ema")

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Delivering Results For Hoosiers
body:
This week marked the end of the 2018 legislative session. Over the last two and a half months, Hoosiers have been center stage at the Statehouse... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7e8e12f02e&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Delivering Results For Hoosiers_** This week marked the end of the 2018 legislative session. Over the last two and a half months, Hoosiers have been center stage at the Statehouse, with a people-focused approach guiding **Governor Eric Holcomb** and our Republican-led legislature to drive solutions that will help save lives and provide greater economic opportunity to people across the state.

Their unwavering focus delivered real results for Hoosiers. For example, one of the biggest issues this session -- and a key priority of Governor Holcomb's -- was building a better system for Indiana's workforce development. Right now, we have 85,000 unfilled jobs in Indiana, and even more to come as our economy continues to grow. Governor Holcomb called this the "defining issue of the decade," and he's absolutely right.

This session, the Indiana General Assembly passed legislation that will be critical to helping us build our 21st Century skilled and ready workforce. It ensures that all Hoosiers, regardless of their stage in life, have access to training that provides a clear pathway to filling good, high-wage jobs -- a big victory for all Hoosiers. This training must align with the needs of real employers, and it must be accessible for all Hoosiers -- employers _and_ employees.

I can't wait to see Governor Holcomb sign this game-changing legislation for Indiana. But legislators' work didn't stop there.

This session, our legislators worked to attack the opioid crisis -- including bringing addiction treatment centers closer to all Hoosiers -- and got Hoosier students involved in their government like never before by standing by them as they advocated for a new state insect.

While there's always more work to be done, Indiana stands as a national leader in so many ways, and it's because of the Republican leaders we have working for us at the Statehouse. With Indiana's Party of Purpose at the helm, we'll keep on delivering real results for Hoosiers.

-Kyle Hupfer
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| **_Hoosiers Win in 2018 Legislative Session_**
Now that the 2018 legislative session has ended, it's bill-signing season. And luckily for Governor Holcomb, he's got a few busy weeks ahead of him signing successful bills that were key parts of his 2018 Next Level Legislative Agenda, including:

**Providing Indiana's Tech Sector with the Tools to Grow**
Indiana becomes one of only four states to exempt Software as a Service from sales tax – helping spur the growth of high-wage jobs in Indiana’s tech sector.

**Providing All Hoosier Students with Computer Training**
As technology is driving growth in most of Indiana’s major industries, students at all Indiana schools will soon have access to computer science courses.

**Quickly**** Preparing Hoosiers for High-Demand, High-Wage Jobs**
Indiana will assess our current workforce programs, align them better with the needs of real job creators, and make sure our programs are accessible and easy-to-use for both employees and employers.

**Improving Data in the Fight Against Opioids**
These bills work to build more accurate and standardized local information on drug overdose deaths (helping lead to better decision-making with the crisis) and provide health care professionals with a system to check which medications have already been prescribed to Hoosiers (better controlling the flow of addictive drugs).

**Expanding Access to Addiction Treatment**
Expands the number of opioid treatment centers to ensure nearly every Hoosier is within an hour's drive of help

**Working to Reduce Indiana’s Infant Mortality Rate**
Standardizes health facility levels of care to make sure treatment is provided at a facility that meets the needs of mother and baby

**Engaging Hundreds of Hoosier Students in their Government**
Worked with Hoosier students to designate the Say’s Firefly as Indiana’s official state insect

---
|
---
|
---
| **_America's Economy Soars With President Trump_** Our president continues to impress us with big wins! In February, the U.S. added 313,000 jobs \-- thanks to a booming national economy and **President Donald Trump**'s tax cuts that are putting more money in Hoosier pockets. The economy is moving forward in America!** **
* National unemployment is at 4.1%, a 17-year low.
* More than 4 million Americans are earning bonuses this year thanks to tax reform
* 80 million Americans are seeing lower utility bills
* Consumer confidence is at its highest level since November 2000

Hoosiers are really noticing the impact President Trump is having on our nation's economic growth!
* A recent poll shows that 54% of Hoosiers think that the economy is stronger today than it was a year ago
* 59% of Hoosiers approve of President Trump's tax cuts

Doesn't this all sound great? Then why did Democrat Joe Donnelly vote against tax reform?

clean_body: This week marked the end of the 2018 legislative session. Over the last two and a half months, Hoosiers have been center stage at the Statehouse... | | | | | | | Delivering Results For Hoosiers This week marked the end of the 2018 legislative session. Over the last two and a half months, Hoosiers have been center stage at the Statehouse, with a people-focused approach guiding Governor Eric Holcomb and our Republican-led legislature to drive solutions that will help save lives and provide greater economic opportunity to people across the state. Their unwavering focus delivered real results for Hoosiers. For example, one of the biggest issues this session -- and a key priority of Governor Holcomb's -- was building a better system for Indiana's workforce development. Right now, we have 85,000 unfilled jobs in Indiana, and even more to come as our economy continues to grow. Governor Holcomb called this the "defining issue of the decade," and he's absolutely right. This session, the Indiana General Assembly passed legislation that will be critical to helping us build our 21st Century skilled and ready workforce. It ensures that all Hoosiers, regardless of their stage in life, have access to training that provides a clear pathway to filling good, high-wage jobs -- a big victory for all Hoosiers. This training must align with the needs of real employers, and it must be accessible for all Hoosiers -- employers and employees. I can't wait to see Governor Holcomb sign this game-changing legislation for Indiana. But legislators' work didn't stop there. This session, our legislators worked to attack the opioid crisis -- including bringing addiction treatment centers closer to all Hoosiers -- and got Hoosier students involved in their government like never before by standing by them as they advocated for a new state insect. While there's always more work to be done, Indiana stands as a national leader in so many ways, and it's because of the Republican leaders we have working for us at the Statehouse. With Indiana's Party of Purpose at the helm, we'll keep on delivering real results for Hoosiers. -Kyle Hupfer | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Hoosiers Win in 2018 Legislative Session Now that the 2018 legislative session has ended, it's bill-signing season. And luckily for Governor Holcomb, he's got a few busy weeks ahead of him signing successful bills that were key parts of his 2018 Next Level Legislative Agenda, including: Providing Indiana's Tech Sector with the Tools to Grow Indiana becomes one of only four states to exempt Software as a Service from sales tax – helping spur the growth of high-wage jobs in Indiana’s tech sector. Providing All Hoosier Students with Computer Training As technology is driving growth in most of Indiana’s major industries, students at all Indiana schools will soon have access to computer science courses. Quickly Preparing Hoosiers for High-Demand, High-Wage Jobs Indiana will assess our current workforce programs, align them better with the needs of real job creators, and make sure our programs are accessible and easy-to-use for both employees and employers. Improving Data in the Fight Against Opioids These bills work to build more accurate and standardized local information on drug overdose deaths (helping lead to better decision-making with the crisis) and provide health care professionals with a system to check which medications have already been prescribed to Hoosiers (better controlling the flow of addictive drugs). Expanding Access to Addiction Treatment Expands the number of opioid treatment centers to ensure nearly every Hoosier is within an hour's drive of help Working to Reduce Indiana’s Infant Mortality Rate Standardizes health facility levels of care to make sure treatment is provided at a facility that meets the needs of mother and baby Engaging Hundreds of Hoosier Students in their Government Worked with Hoosier students to designate the Say’s Firefly as Indiana’s official state insect | | | America's Economy Soars With President Trump Our president continues to impress us with big wins! In February, the U.S. added 313,000 jobs \-- thanks to a booming national economy and President Donald Trump's tax cuts that are putting more money in Hoosier pockets. The economy is moving forward in America! National unemployment is at 4.1%, a 17-year low. More than 4 million Americans are earning bonuses this year thanks to tax reform 80 million Americans are seeing lower utility bills Consumer confidence is at its highest level since November 2000 Hoosiers are really noticing the impact President Trump is having on our nation's economic growth! A recent poll shows that 54% of Hoosiers think that the economy is stronger today than it was a year ago 59% of Hoosiers approve of President Trump's tax cuts Doesn't this all sound great?
